steps.title: Identify the contrast in apparent distance
steps.content: The key signal lies in each speaker's treatment of the moon's position. In Text 1, “What seemed so far away / Is but a child's balloon”—the moon, which ordinarily appears distant, is revealed to be remarkably close, tangled right above in the ship's mast. In Text 2, the emphasis is reversed: “up so high” and “Will you come down soon?” repeatedly underscore the moon's distance and unreachability.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content: Choice D captures the essential contrast: Text 1 presents the moon as seeming very close (the sudden discovery of proximity in the mast), while Text 2 emphasizes distance (the moon “up so high,” unreachable). The other choices mischaracterize the poems—A mistakenly sees entrapment in both texts, B ignores Text 1's dismissive tone, and C confuses Text 2's affectionate address for serious study. That matches choice D — D is correct.
